heading: Community food and nutrition programs
label: 9922 Community food and nutrition programs
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior was classified to , prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .section 681 of Pub. L. 97–35section 9910 of this titlePub. L. 105–285

Change of NameCommittee on Labor and Human Resources of Senate changed to Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of Senate by Senate Resolution No. 20, One Hundred Sixth Congress, .Jan. 19, 1999
amendments: Prior ProvisionsA prior was classified to , prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .section 681 of Pub. L. 97–35section 9910 of this titlePub. L. 105–285

children.text: to coordinate private and public food assistance resources, wherever the grant recipient involved determines such coordination to be inadequate, to better serve low-income populations;
to assist low-income communities to identify potential sponsors of child nutrition programs and to initiate such programs in underserved or unserved areas; and
to develop innovative approaches at the State and local level to meet the nutrition needs of low-income individuals.

children.heading: Allotments and distribution of funds
children.label: b Allotments and distribution of funds

children.heading: Allotments
children.label: A Allotments
children.text: From a portion equal to 60 percent of such amount (but not to exceed $3,600,000), the Secretary shall allot for grants to eligible agencies for statewide programs in each State the amount that bears the same ratio to such portion as the low-income and unemployed population of such State bears to the low-income and unemployed population of all the States.

children.heading: Competitive grants
children.label: B Competitive grants
children.text: From a portion equal to 40 percent of such amount (but not to exceed $2,400,000), the Secretary shall make grants on a competitive basis to eligible agencies for local and statewide programs.

children.heading: Allotments
children.label: A Allotments
children.text: The Secretary shall use 40 percent of such excess to allot for grants under subsection (a) to eligible agencies for statewide programs in each State an amount that bears the same ratio to 40 percent of such excess as the low-income and unemployed population of such State bears to the low-income and unemployed population of all the States.

children.heading: Competitive grants for local and statewide programs
children.label: B Competitive grants for local and statewide programs
children.text: The Secretary shall use 40 percent of such excess to make grants under subsection (a) on a competitive basis to eligible agencies for local and statewide programs.

children.heading: Competitive grants for nationwide programs
children.label: C Competitive grants for nationwide programs
children.text: The Secretary shall use the remaining 20 percent of such excess to make grants under subsection (a) on a competitive basis to eligible agencies for nationwide programs, including programs benefiting Indians, as defined in , and migrant or seasonal farmworkers.section 9911 of this title

children.heading: Eligibility for allotments for statewide programs
children.label: 3 Eligibility for allotments for statewide programs
children.text: To be eligible to receive an allotment under paragraph (1)(A) or (2)(A), an eligible agency shall demonstrate that the proposed program is statewide in scope and represents a comprehensive and coordinated effort to alleviate hunger within the State.

children.label: i
children.text: $15,000 if the total amount appropriated to carry out this section is not less than $7,000,000 but less than $10,000,000;

children.label: ii
children.text: $20,000 if the total amount appropriated to carry out this section is not less than $10,000,000 but less than $15,000,000; or

children.label: iii
children.text: $30,000 if the total amount appropriated to carry out this section is not less than $15,000,000.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: B Definition
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “State” does not include Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: Maximum grants
children.label: 5 Maximum grants
children.text: From funds made available under paragraphs (1)(B) and (2)(B) for any fiscal year, the Secretary may not make grants under subsection (a) to an eligible agency in an aggregate amount exceeding $50,000. From funds made available under paragraph (2)(C) for any fiscal year, the Secretary may not make grants under subsection (a) to an eligible agency in an aggregate amount exceeding $300,000.
information on the amount of funding awarded to each grant recipient; and

a summary of the activities performed by the grant recipients with funding awarded under this section and a description of the manner in which such activities meet the objectives described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 1999 through 2003.
